intent: Ship the whisper-writer streaming rewrite so voice-to-text keystrokes feel instant.
finding: Work on whisper-writer crossed local midnight on three consecutive near-nights (Apr 7 23:26->00:01, Apr 14 22:20->00:14, Apr 14 23:44->00:33), with a follow-on session opened at 00:15 on Apr 15 that continued iterating on transcription performance. The Apr 14 session kept 123 user messages of contiguous activity past midnight. This is the wellbeing-category *manic mode* signature -- late-night work after 23:00 and scope that carries across the midnight boundary -- seen three times in ten days on the same project. Pattern spans 3 occurrences without explicit user fatigue text, so placed at medium rather than critical per the severity anchors.
existing_mitigation: None found. Global CLAUDE.md has no time-of-day bound, hour-aware hook, or break reminder. No confirmed wellbeing off-track pattern exists in ~/.claude/projects/-home-jonathan--claude/memory/ yet.

intent: Keep stacking features on the Claude harness (voice loop, date-night cron, skill scheduling, hooks) into late evenings.
finding: On the evening of Apr 11, a new large scope was opened at 23:35 ('Voice loop: verify Piper TTS end-to-end') and another at 23:47 ('schedule a skill creation at 01:00') -- i.e. the user was not only working past 23:00 but actively scheduling new work into the small hours. That evening logged 1441 user messages between 20:00-06:00 local across 7 sessions, with 263 of them after 23:00. This fits the *scope escalating mid-session* and *short intense bursts* manic-mode signatures from categories.md. Notable that the user explicitly asked Claude to schedule more work at 01:00 rather than next morning -- that is the wellbeing-relevant signal, not the late hour alone.
existing_mitigation: None found. The user does have a TickTick CLI (~/.claude/todo-add) and a weekly date-night cron per memory/MEMORY.md, but nothing that flags 'this is a late-night scope escalation' at the moment of scheduling.

intent: Maintain many parallel Claude investigations (security hardening, nixos, whisper-writer, autodoro, skills review) within a single day.
finding: Real (non-stub, non-programmatic) user-driven session starts peaked at 34 on Apr 11, 29 on Apr 14, and 24 on Apr 12 -- roughly 3x the median of prior days. Many of these are short sessions within the same hour on different topics, which matches the *many short exchanges, scope escalating* manic-mode signature rather than focused deep work. Flagging at low because the user has not confirmed this as off-track in memory and because high session count may also reflect intentional multi-repo exploration days; the pattern is worth noting so repeat days can be compared against it.
